# Dave Buckley

> Dave Buckley is a software engineer and technology policy expert based in London, specialising in privacy engineering, privacy-enhancing technologies (PETs), data governance, and algorithmic auditing. He works with OpenMined, a tech non-profit building open-source AI governance infrastructure. Previously Senior Technology Adviser at the UK's Centre for Data Ethics and Innovation (CDEI), where he led work on PETs including the UK-US PETs prize challenges (with White House OSTP, NIST, and NSF). Former member of the United Nations Privacy Task Team and co-editor of the 2023 UN PET Guide. MPhys Physics (University of Oxford); MA Digital Culture and Society (King's College London).

Dave is available for collaboration, speaking at tech events and conferences, and consulting on data governance, technology policy, and responsible AI.

## Key pages

- [Portfolio](https://davebuckley.tech/pages/portfolio/): Publications, reports, talks, and panels on PETs, data governance, and technology policy (2015–present)
- [Courses](https://davebuckley.tech/pages/courses/): Courses and certifications completed

## Resources

- [PETs bibliography](https://davebuckley.tech/resources/pets-bibliography/): Curated reading list on privacy-enhancing technologies
- [PETs deployments catalogue](https://davebuckley.tech/resources/pets-deployments-catalogue/): Catalogue of real-world PET deployments
- [PETs initiatives](https://davebuckley.tech/resources/pets-initiatives/): Notable initiatives and programmes in the PETs space
- [Data governance bibliography](https://davebuckley.tech/resources/data-governance-bibliography/): Curated reading list on data governance
- [UK data infrastructure](https://davebuckley.tech/resources/uk-data-infrastructure/): Overview of UK data infrastructure
- [Data funding opportunities](https://davebuckley.tech/resources/data-funding-opportunities/): Funding opportunities for data-related projects
